skill.name: caveman-commit
skill.description: Generates concise Conventional Commit messages from staged changes or a described diff. It keeps the subject short and adds a body only when context is needed.
skill.summary: Creates terse Conventional Commit messages with optional context bodies.
skill.author: juliusbrussee

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks and a weak-crypto keyword match. Review found no executable code, shell invocation, network access, filesystem mutation, prompt injection, or cryptographic operation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Command Examples
low_findings.description: The external command findings are inline Markdown examples and commit message samples. The skill explicitly says it only generates a message and does not run git commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is documentation and example commit output, not executable code. Line 65 states that the skill does not run git commit, stage files, or amend commits.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Keyword Match
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm finding points to the YAML description field. No cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive hashing operation appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The finding is located in front matter text that describes the skill. There is no code path or instruction involving encryption, hashing, or deprecated algorithms.

content.value_statement: Verbose commit messages slow reviews and hide the reason for a change. This skill creates short Conventional Commit messages with context only when it matters.
actual_capabilities: Writes Conventional Commit subjects using common types such as feat, fix, docs, and refactor.
Keeps subject lines short, with a hard cap guidance of 72 characters.
Adds a body for breaking changes, migrations, security fixes, and non-obvious reasons.
Uses issue and pull request references at the end of the message when provided.
Avoids AI attribution, first-person phrasing, emoji, and redundant file names.
limitations: Does not run git commit, stage files, amend commits, or modify the repository.
Depends on the provided diff or staged change context to infer intent.
May need user input when the reason for a change is not visible in the diff.
Does not replace project-specific release or changelog policy.

use_cases.title: Prepare a focused commit
use_cases.description: Generate a compact commit message after finishing a small code change.

use_cases.title: Document risky changes
use_cases.description: Add the right body text for breaking changes, migrations, and security fixes.

use_cases.title: Standardize team history
use_cases.description: Keep commit subjects aligned with Conventional Commits across contributors.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Commit Message
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a commit message for my staged changes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after staging a simple change.

prompt_templates.title: Commit From Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Conventional Commit message for this change: I fixed empty search results by handling null filters.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the diff is unavailable but the change is clear.

prompt_templates.title: Breaking Change Commit
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a commit message for a breaking API change. Include a body with migration notes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when users must change their code or configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Review Before Commit
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the staged diff and write the shortest accurate Conventional Commit message. Ask if the reason is unclear.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for complex changes where intent may not be obvious.

output_examples.input: A bug fix prevents duplicate invoices when a payment webhook is retried.
An API route was renamed and clients must migrate before the old route is removed.
output_examples.output:
feat(api)!: rename orders route

BREAKING CHANGE: clients must migrate to the new checkout route before the old route is removed.

output_examples.input: Documentation adds setup steps for local PostgreSQL and seed data.
output_examples.output: docs: add local database setup steps

best_practices: Stage related changes together before asking for a commit message.
Provide the reason for the change when the diff only shows implementation details.
Keep the generated message aligned with the repository's existing commit style.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to combine unrelated changes into one vague commit.
Do not remove body text from breaking changes, migrations, or security fixes.
Do not ask it to run git commands or mutate repository state.

faq.question: Does this skill create commits automatically?
faq.answer: No. It only writes the commit message text for you to review and use.

faq.question: Does it support Conventional Commits?
faq.answer: Yes. It formats messages with a type, optional scope, concise subject, and optional body.

faq.question: When will it add a body?
faq.answer: It adds a body for non-obvious reasons, breaking changes, migrations, linked issues, security fixes, and reverts.

faq.question: Can it infer everything from a diff?
faq.answer: No. It may ask for the reason when intent is not clear from the changed files.

faq.question: Will it add AI attribution?
faq.answer: No. The skill instructs the assistant to avoid generated-by attribution in commit messages.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: The skill contains no network or command execution behavior. Review your AI tool settings before sharing private diffs.
